architecture_readout: Actual-command context is actionable only when it is isolated from unsafe labels.
Non-actual command keywords are useful alpha for noise filtering, not for weakening safety boundaries.
Weak unresolved proxies and user pushback are control gates before adoption scoring.
Resolved false and failed success proxies are negative outcome signals, not weak positive adoption evidence.
Profile selection should keep architecture gates ahead of raw average lift.

pairwise_contrasts.readout: Independent actual-command samples are the real proof for the command threshold; unsafe-label-confounded samples are supporting evidence only.
This separates command-looking text that should be filtered from actual commands that must keep boundary review.
Weak unresolved evidence should behave like holdout pressure, while resolved true evidence can support acceptance after gates.
User pushback is negative/rejection-map evidence and should not be averaged away by adoption count.
Resolved false evidence is a separate negative outcome signal; it should not share the positive adoption path.
Benign command execution context may be useful workload alpha, but risky actual commands keep the hard boundary.

benign_actual_command_context.interpretation: Benign actual commands prove the classifier can see real execution, but they do not prove safety or success by themselves.

high_tool_activity.interpretation: High tool activity is useful pressure for deeper review, not acceptance, success, or safety authority.

non_actual_command_pattern_alpha.interpretation: Command patterns in tool output, plans, instructions, and quoted logs are useful as noise evidence only.

promoted_alpha.allowed_use: support noise filtering for command keywords in tool output, plans, instructions, quoted logs, and similar non-execution contexts
promoted_alpha.blocked_use: do not weaken unsafe boundaries, actual-command boundaries, route authority, or winner authority

promoted_alpha.allowed_use: raise review depth or evidence requirements for complex traces
promoted_alpha.blocked_use: do not treat high tool volume as success evidence, safety evidence, or adoption authority

diagnostic_only.allowed_use: measure command parser coverage and separate benign execution from risky execution
diagnostic_only.blocked_use: do not use benign commands to lower actual-risk thresholds or accept work without outcome evidence

candidates.allowed_use: raise damping and negative-outcome review pressure when failure evidence exists without relying on unsafe labels
candidates.blocked_use: do not treat failed outcome as route authority, winner authority, or production rejection without review

candidates.allowed_use: raise evidence-budget pressure when command-looking noise overlaps with failed outcome evidence
candidates.blocked_use: do not convert command noise plus failure into automatic rejection or route changes

candidates.allowed_use: raise review depth when weak unresolved proxy evidence appears inside complex traces
candidates.blocked_use: do not use tool volume plus weak proxy as success, safety, route, or winner evidence

candidates.allowed_use: raise rejection-ledger and damping pressure when user pushback overlaps with weak or failed proxy evidence
candidates.blocked_use: do not let pushback alone become winner authority or permanent negative memory

candidates.allowed_use: track install/network command-looking traces as source-scoped complexity pressure
candidates.blocked_use: do not promote until the signal survives outside a single source family
